body.text: For some dumb reason, I got married — hastily — before I deployed.
That went exactly like you'd expect.
While I was in enemy skies over hostile territory, he was hanging out with a new girlfriend.
You can imagine, I was thrilled to find that out.
After a long 100 days in the hot desert — in the middle of summer — I came home: to a destroyed house and a marriage that was about to be the same.
Then it happened for the first time: he tackled me around the ankles — as I was trying to leave the fight — and my ankle broke. Snap.
We were young and stupid, so remorse was felt — and we tried to make it work.
Not too long later, the second incident came: he punched me the jaw. I retaliated. The cops were called. We both ended up in jail for the night.
We both ended up in jail for the night.
Fortunately, he admitted to being the aggressor. I was let go and he got misdemeanor domestic abuse.
Again, more remorse and more attempts to reconcile. All the while, my bright military career fading.
Group counseling. Individual counseling. Therapy. On repeat. We tried.
That was when I had to make the hardest decision of my life.

credentials.label: Airmen led as Chief of Security
